description:
You are acting as a DevSecOps engineer who must codify a very small ­file-system-permission policy inside your own home directory.

  Goal
  ----
  1. Create two sibling directories.
     • Absolute paths to create:
       /home/user/policy_demo/confidential
       /home/user/policy_demo/public

  2. Apply the following permission policy (octal notation):
       /home/user/policy_demo/confidential  →  700
       /home/user/policy_demo/public        →  755

     NOTE: Only standard Unix permissions (chmod) are required—no ACLs, no sudo.

  3. Produce an **audit log** proving the enforcement.
     • The log must be a plain-text file named
       /home/user/policy_demo/permission_audit.log
     • It must contain exactly two non-blank lines, **in this order**:
         confidential:700
         public:755
       No leading/trailing spaces, no extra newlines.

  Output verification criteria
  ----------------------------
  Automated tests will confirm ALL of the following:

  A. Both directories exist at the precise absolute paths given above.  
  B. The *mode bits* (ls -ld) match the octal values specified (owner rwx, group & others per octal).  
  C. /home/user/policy_demo/permission_audit.log exists, is a regular file, and its contents are byte-for-byte identical to

         confidential:700
         public:755

  Implementation tips (not commands)
  ----------------------------------
  • Feel free to create the parent directory policy_demo if it doesn’t exist.  
  • Use any combination of mkdir and chmod to meet the policy.  
  • After setting permissions, generate the audit log exactly as described.  
  • A total of 2–3 shell commands are sufficient.  
  • Do all work as the default non-privileged user.

  Once everything is in place, the task is complete.
truth:
EXPECTED_FINAL_STATE:
    Directories:
      - path: /home/user/policy_demo/confidential
        mode: "700"
      - path: /home/user/policy_demo/public
        mode: "755"
    Log_File:
      path: /home/user/policy_demo/permission_audit.log
      contents: |
        confidential:700
        public:755
name: task_000000_b92e706e
